chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.S-69 of 2026. (Abdul Samad vs P.O.Sindh and others) Date Order with the signature of the Judge 1. For orders on CMA No.226/2026 2. For orders on office objections 3. For orders on CMA No.227/2026 4. For the hearing of the main case. 18.03.2026 Mr. Asadullah Soomro, advocate holds brief for Syed Zafar Ali Shah Bukhari, Advocate for the Petitioner. ***************** 1. Granted. 2to4. During perusal of the record, it transpires that the petitioner had already approached the learned Justice of Peace by filing an application under Sections 22-A & 22-B, Cr.P.C., which was disposed of vide order dated 04.08.2025, whereby protection was granted. Since the purpose has already been achieved, the subsequent proceedings initiated by invoking the extraordinary Constitutional jurisdiction of this Court are not maintainable, as the petitioner has already been extended protection in the shape of a constitutional right by the learned Justice of Peace. Even otherwise, no case of harassment or unnecessary interference has been made out, in view of the dictum laid down in Abdul Hameed’s case reported as PLD 2019 Sindh 138. Such view has also been endorsed by this Court, Circuit at Larkana, in Cr. Misc. Application No. 282 of 2026. Accordingly, this petition, being devoid of merits, is hereby dismissed in limine, along with all pending applications. JUDGE
